title: Tragacantha caprina
taxonomy: Tragacantha caprina is classified under the family Fabaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Faboideae, which Tragacantha caprina belongs to. The genus Tragacantha is a part of the subfamily Faboideae, and it is closely related to other genera such as Astragalus and Oxytropis. Tragacantha caprina is a species of shrub or small tree that is native to certain regions. It is a member of the order Fabales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes many species of legumes.

Tragacantha caprina, also known as Goat's Thorn or Goat's Head, is a plant species with various medicinal uses. It has been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues and is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dysentery, and stomach ulcers. Additionally, Tragacantha caprina is thought to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for wound infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Tragacantha caprina for medicinal purposes.
